summary: Frontend-only UI test of Hoshang Sheth Liquid Glass portfolio at the public preview URL. All 50+ data-testids present, hero/about/capabilities/projects/journey/stack/exploring/contact/footer all render correctly, project links point to correct external URLs with target=_blank and rel=noreferrer, nav anchors scroll-to-section works, mobile menu toggle works at 390px viewport, IntersectionObserver reveal animations fire (11/11 .reveal elements inside Stack get .in class after scroll). No console errors and no page errors. Form uses JS-handled fetch to formspree.io/f/xojrkygl (not native form action attribute) — verified in source code; submit button is enabled and not disabled.
critical_code_review_comments: Portfolio.jsx is 1163 lines — recommend splitting into per-section files (Nav.jsx, Hero.jsx, About.jsx, Projects.jsx, Journey.jsx, Stack.jsx, Exploring.jsx, Contact.jsx, Footer.jsx) and a data/portfolioData.js for content arrays. Improves maintainability.
Unused FloatCard component (lines 444-461) — only FloatCardStatic is used. Dead code can be removed.

Form submits to Formspree via fetch — note the review request mentioned 'verify form action attribute' but the implementation uses onSubmit JS handler, not the native action attribute. This is functionally equivalent and intentional but worth documenting if a future test agent expects action='https://formspree.io/...'.

Reveal class delay variants use `i % 5` and `i % 4` modulo logic which can produce 'd0' (no class added when i%n===0); this appears intentional to stagger the first card with no delay, but produces inconsistent class names across sections — fine functionally.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pure frontend portfolio. Backend not used. Public URL: https://projects-modal.preview.emergentagent.com. All data-testids verified present. Reveal animations gated behind IntersectionObserver in /app/frontend/src/hooks/useReveal.js with a 2.5s safety-net fallback. Contact form posts via JS fetch to https://formspree.io/f/xojrkygl (do NOT actually submit during tests).
rca of the issue: No issues found — all listed acceptance criteria pass.
